exploration.title: Multi-Provider LLM Dispatch + Graceful Fallback
openQuestions: Q1: Scope boundary — declarative dispatch/registry surface above ExecutionPlan vs also owning the shared retry/Schedule + round-robin + circuit-breaker stack?
Q2: Build-vs-buy — reuse vendored ExecutionPlan + the four existing drivers and clean-room the glue vs build/port a bespoke fallback engine?
Q3: First slice — Anthropic→single-fallback over the four installed drivers wired into AnthropicTurnKernel vs a broader provider set/consumer?
Q4: Registry shape — adapter table over the four non-uniform driver surfaces (reading driver config constants) vs a uniform driver.model() registry?
Q5: Vendor/auth — key precedence (user>CLI>env), advisory prefix detect, missing-primary-key advance-vs-fail-fast, and broker ApiKeyResolver?
Q6: Package placement — new @beep driver-tier dispatch package vs foundation/capability vs inline in agents/server?
Q7: V1 resilience scope — ordered fallback only (defer round-robin + circuit-breaker) and reuse generateObject for structured output vs rebuild?
